posture: Quiet Friday scan — no confirmable new, in-scope innovation signal surfaced in the last 24 hours. The sector's active frontiers (FOGO/circular economy, AI-assisted development assessment, smart communities) remain warm but produced no fresh, in-window council announcement today; the strongest recent 'adopt' material remains the 2026 National Awards for Local Government already logged on 29 June.
summary: Empty day. A full daily scan of the free §1a-§1d sources (AU council & sector press, AU industry verticals, overseas equal-weight outlets and the tertiary/research institutes) returned no item that was simultaneously (a) published in the last 24 hours, (b) in scope per the §3b Australian-council-function filter, and (c) not already covered in the previous 3 days. As this is a Friday, the §1e 50-council watchlist deep scan was not run (Saturdays only). Several recurring or undated items were again considered and excluded: the NSW statewide FOGO mandate (business-tier obligations commencing 1 July) is a regulatory-rollout milestone, not a fresh innovation signal; Hawkesbury City Council's ARC Ento Tech black-soldier-fly waste-to-resource trial resurfaced only via older (November 2025) coverage; the NSW AI-in-planning tools (DAISY, myLot with Penrith/Wollondilly) produced no new dated development; Townsville's AI pedestrian-detection waste-truck safety trial dates to June and is out-of-window; and overseas material (Bloomberg Mayors Challenge winners, UK/EU Local Digital Twins for Smart Communities call, Milton Keynes/Harrow drone digital twins) was either out-of-window, a funding/awards notice rather than a transferable council signal, or general policy. No paywalled items were logged; where a paid title was the only source it was held to headline + standfirst and not used.
